title: verbascum speciosum subsp. megaphlomos
intro: Verbascum speciosum subsp. megaphlomos, also known as the large-flowered mullein or mullein, is a plant species that belongs to the mullein genus, known for its large, showy flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, verbascoside, and mullein glycosides, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Verbascum speciosum subsp. megaphlomos include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and wounds. Herbal preparations of Verbascum speciosum subsp. megaphlomos can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
